factors.summary: The company's debt leverage is stable as it maintains compliance with its debt covenants.

evidence.text: As of November 30, 2018, Cintas was in compliance with all debt covenants.

factors.summary: The company is actively returning capital to shareholders through share buybacks.

evidence.text: During the six months ended November 30, 2018, under the August 2, 2016 plan, we purchased a total of 2.1 million shares of Cintas common stock at an average price of $192.55 for a total purchase price of $410.0 million. This completed the August 2, 2016 share buyback program.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is stable as operating activities continue to provide significant liquidity.

evidence.text: Cash flows provided by operating activities have historically supplied us with a significant source of liquidity.

factors.summary: The company's litigation exposure is not material and does not pose a significant risk.

evidence.text: Cintas is subject to legal proceedings, insurance receipts, legal settlements and claims arising from the ordinary course of its business... the aggregate liability, if any, with respect to such ordinary course of business actions will not have a material adverse effect...

factors.summary: Overall demand is accelerating, driven by increased sales volume and customer activity.

evidence.text: Total revenue increased 7.0% for the three months ended November 30, 2018 over the same period in the prior fiscal year, from $1,606.4 million to $1,718.3 million. Revenue increased organically by 7.0% as a result of increased sales volume.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended November 30, 2018 Compared to Three Months Ended November 30, 2017

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is accelerating, with organic growth rates of 7.0% and 6.1% for the three and six months ended November 30, 2018, respectively.

evidence.text: Revenue increased organically by 7.0% as a result of increased sales volume. [...] Revenue increased organically by 6.1% as a result of increased sales volume.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended November 30, 2018 Compared to Three Months Ended November 30, 2017

factors.summary: Unit volumes and transaction counts are growing, driven by increased sales volume across both reportable segments.

evidence.text: Uniform Rental and Facility Services reportable operating segment revenue increased [...] driven by many factors including new business sold by sales representatives, penetration of additional products and services into existing customers and strong customer retention. [...] First Aid and Safety Services reportable operating segment revenue increased [...] as a result of increased sales volume.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended November 30, 2018 Compared to Three Months Ended November 30, 2017

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments, particularly in the First Aid and Safety Services, which has a higher gross margin percentage compared to previous periods.

evidence.text: The gross margin as a percent of revenue was 48.0% for the quarter ended November 30, 2018, which is an increase of 110 basis points compared to the gross margin as a percent of revenue of 46.9% in the same period of the prior fiscal year.
evidence.subsection: Six Months Ended November 30, 2018 Compared to Six Months Ended November 30, 2017

factors.summary: Gross margins are expanding, driven by cost efficiency improvements and production optimizations.

evidence.text: The gross margin was 60 basis points higher than the prior fiscal year’s second quarter gross margin of 44.7%. [...] The increase was driven by lower production related supplies expenses and improvements in process efficiency.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended November 30, 2018 Compared to Three Months Ended November 30, 2017

factors.summary: Input costs are stable or declining, with lower production related supplies expenses contributing to margin improvements.

evidence.text: The increase was driven by lower production related supplies expenses and improvements in process efficiency. [...] The gross margin was 10 basis points higher than the first six months of the prior fiscal year’s gross margin of 45.4%. The increase was driven by lower production related supplies expenses.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended November 30, 2018 Compared to Three Months Ended November 30, 2017

factors.summary: Labor costs are manageable, with only minor increases in employee-related expenses that have not significantly impacted margins.

evidence.text: The majority of the increase was due to higher Uniform Rental and Facility Services and First Aid and Safety Services reportable operating segment sales volume, as well as increased employee-partner related expenses. [...] The increase in selling and administrative expenses as a percent of revenue was due to higher employee-partner related expenses.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended November 30, 2018 Compared to Three Months Ended November 30, 2017

factors.summary: Competitive dynamics are favorable, with the company successfully integrating acquisitions and maintaining market leadership.

evidence.text: Cintas completed the acquisition of G&K Services, Inc. (G&K) for consideration of approximately $2.1 billion. G&K is now a wholly-owned subsidiary of Cintas that operates within the Uniform Rental and Facility Services operating segment. [...] The company is pursuing the strategy of broadening its customer base through geographic expansion and strategic acquisitions.

factors.summary: Market share is improving, particularly in the First Aid and Safety Services segment, with organic growth and successful integration of acquired businesses.

evidence.text: First Aid and Safety Services reportable operating segment revenue increased [...] as a result of increased sales volume and was positively impacted by 0.1% due to acquisitions. [...] The company is North America’s leading provider of corporate identity uniforms through rental and sales programs.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended November 30, 2018 Compared to Three Months Ended November 30, 2017

factors.summary: Management has maintained positive forward guidance, with expectations for continued revenue growth and margin expansion.

evidence.text: Net income from continuing operations for the three months ended November 30, 2018 increased $105.3 million, or 76.4%, compared to the three months ended November 30, 2017. [...] Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations increased due to the increase in earnings from continuing operations.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended November 30, 2018 Compared to Three Months Ended November 30, 2017

factors.summary: Capital expenditure is focused on supporting growth initiatives, with a positive outlook for future expansion.

evidence.text: The company is pursuing the strategy of broadening its customer base through geographic expansion and strategic acquisitions. [...] The company is investing in operational efficiency improvements and infrastructure to support continued growth.
